reasoning: Since no web search tools are available, I must base my estimate on base rates and the structure of poker tournaments. The National Heads-Up Poker Championship historically featured 64 players. In a heads-up format, variance is high, and even for an elite player like Kristen Foxen, the probability of stringing together six straight wins (if a 64-player field) is only marginally better than pure chance (1/64 = ~1.56%). Factoring in some likelihood of her being a skilled favorite, her odds are possibly around 2-3%, but it's also possible she might not be playing, or has already played and faced elimination. Given the available options and the strict constraint of minimum probability inputs (0.02), an estimate of 2% is the logical baseline.
